name: About Mole
recipeIngredient: Mole (pronounced mole-a) is a series of Mexican sauces that contain ground chiles, spices, nuts, often chocolate, sometimes raisins, ground seeds, etc.
recipeInstructions:
There are three basic types of moles:
1. Mole Poblano (the most famous type, and the one that ALWAYS contains chocolate) was originated in Pueblo during Colonial times by the nuns who wanted to make a special dish for a visiting Archbishop. The sauce contains ground dried chile peppers, ground nuts, ground raisins, broth, chocolate, sometimes ground corn tortillas, a small amount of sugar, and various spices. It is traditionally served over turkey, with a side dish of unfilled tamales (just the cornmeal masa steamed in corn shucks).
2. Mole Verde (green mole) contains green chiles, broth, ground pumpkin seeds, various herbs and spices.
3. Mole Roja (red mole) is a sauce that contains red chiles, herbs and spices, ground nuts or seeds, ground corn tortillas, usually no chocolate.
All of these dishes are virtual throwbacks to the complex (and to our palates unusual) combinations of ingredients that were common in that part of the country before the arrival of the Spaniards. None of the dishes is particularly hot, they have a complex, haunting flavor that speaks of cultures long gone, but not entirely forgotten.
